title: Java Equals HashCode Comparable
description: Practice object equality, hash codes, and natural ordering.
learningGoals: Recall that equal objects must return equal hash codes.
Recognize that compareTo returns a sign, not a boolean.

completionChecks.prompt: What does new User(7).compareTo(new User(7)) return?
choices: Compilation fails because compareTo must return boolean
completionChecks.explanation: compareTo returns an int sign - negative, zero, or positive. Integer.compare(7, 7) is 0, meaning equal ordering; compareTo never returns a boolean.

completionChecks.prompt: What must be true when two User objects are equal?
choices: Their hashCode results must also be equal
They must be the same object reference
Their compareTo result must be positive
Their ids may differ as long as equals returns true
completionChecks.explanation: The equals/hashCode contract requires that objects equal by equals() return the same hashCode, so equal Users must share a hash code.

choices: Compilation fails because equals cannot accept a String
completionChecks.explanation: equals accepts Object, and the instanceof guard returns false before any cast when the argument is not a User.
